title: Risk Assessment Inputs — Control Deficiency Hotspots

compliance.assurance_rationale: NIST 800-53 RA-3 — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIST 800-53 RA-3 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.14.74: Risk Assessment Inputs — Control Deficiency Hotspots.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.14.74' running on index=compliance, index=vulns, index=sec — use summary joins, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
index=compliance sourcetype=cis:assessment control_result="FAIL" earliest=-30d
| stats count as ctrl_fails by hostname
| join type=left hostname [
    search index=vulns sourcetype=tenable:vuln severity="Critical" earliest=-30d
    | stats count as crit_vulns by host
    | rename host as hostname
  ]
| eval risk_score=ctrl_fails*2 + crit_vulns*5
| sort - risk_score
| head 200
description: Aggregates multiple weak signals (failed controls, vulns, policy violations) by system for RA-3 risk assessment prioritization.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NIST 800-53 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes compliance, sec, vulns and sourcetypes cis:assessment, tenable:vuln. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Replace `join` with `lookup` at scale; (2) calibrate weights annually; (3) export to enterprise risk register; (4) document assumptions. Indexes required: `index=compliance`, `index=sec`, `index=vulns`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=cis:assessment`, `sourcetype=tenable:vuln`.
visualization: Table (top systems), Bubble chart (optional), Single value (mean score). Key fields: `crit_vulns`, `ctrl_fails`, `hostname`, `risk_score`.
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Vulnerabilities.Vulnerabilities by Vulnerabilities.dest | sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
grandmaExplanation: We watch for aggregates multiple weak signals (failed controls, vulns, policy violations) by system for risk assessment prioritization. This matters because it catches problems that could lead to audit findings or compliance gaps.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=compliance`, `index=vulns`, `index=sec` — use summary joins.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Replace `join` with `lookup` at scale; (2) calibrate weights annually; (3) export to enterprise risk register; (4) document assumptions.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=compliance sourcetype=cis:assessment control_result="FAIL" earliest=-30d
| stats count as ctrl_fails by hostname
| join type=left hostname [
    search index=vulns sourcetype=tenable:vuln severity="Critical" earliest=-30d
    | stats count as crit_vulns by host
    | rename host as hostname
  ]
| eval risk_score=ctrl_fails*2 + crit_vulns*5
| sort - risk_score
| head 200
```

Understanding this SPL

**Risk Assessment Inputs — Control Deficiency Hotspots (RA-3)** — Aggregates multiple weak signals (failed controls, vulns, policy violations) by system for RA-3 risk assessment prioritization.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=compliance`, `index=vulns`, `index=sec` — use summary joins. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: compliance; **sourcetype**: cis:assessment. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=compliance, sourcetype=cis:assessment, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` aggregates events by **hostname**, producing one row per unique combination for trend analysis and threshold evaluation.
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **risk_score** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.
• Limits the number of rows with `head`.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Vulnerabilities.Vulnerabilities by Vulnerabilities.dest | sort - count
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Risk Assessment Inputs — Control Deficiency Hotspots (RA-3)** — Aggregates multiple weak signals (failed controls, vulns, policy violations) by system for RA-3 risk assessment prioritization.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=compliance`, `index=vulns`, `index=sec` — use summary joins. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Vulnerabilities.Vulnerabilities` — enable acceleration for that model.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.

Output fields: crit_vulns, ctrl_fails, hostname, risk_score.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (top systems), Bubble chart (optional), Single value (mean score)
